scope: Actual-LI compressed dKV SASG-style split: Cube345/dot-K partial producer followed by a detached Triton ScatterAdd/reduce kernel.

code.change: Added _scatteradd_partials_reduce_kernel and wired single, chunked sequential, and ping-pong stream variants that use a standalone Triton ScatterAdd/reduce stage instead of partials.sum(dim=1).

rerun_commands.s128_smoke: docker run --rm --entrypoint python --device=/dev/kfd --device=/dev/dri --group-add video --ipc=host --network=host --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE --security-opt seccomp=unconfined -e HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 -e PYTHONPATH=/workspace/sources/wip/torchtitan:/workspace/tools/dsv4_runtime_probe -v /scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527:/workspace -w /workspace onenexus/nexus-titan:rocm722-pytorch-nightly-mori tools/dsv4_runtime_probe/benchmark_sparse_mla_shared_kv_sasg_overlap_probe.py --seq-len 128 --layer-id 2 --seq-chunk 16 --pipeline-chunk-tokens 64 --block-d 16 --block-h 64 --block-k 32 --warmup-iters 1 --timed-iters 3 --correctness-atol 0.2 --summary-path /workspace/run_artifacts/sparse_mla_sasg_detached_scatter_s128_layer2_20260603.json --json
rerun_commands.s4096_timing: docker run --rm --entrypoint python --device=/dev/kfd --device=/dev/dri --group-add video --ipc=host --network=host --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE --security-opt seccomp=unconfined -e HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 -e PYTHONPATH=/workspace/sources/wip/torchtitan:/workspace/tools/dsv4_runtime_probe -v /scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527:/workspace -w /workspace onenexus/nexus-titan:rocm722-pytorch-nightly-mori tools/dsv4_runtime_probe/benchmark_sparse_mla_shared_kv_sasg_overlap_probe.py --seq-len 4096 --layer-id 2 --seq-chunk 32 --pipeline-chunk-tokens 512 --block-d 16 --block-h 64 --block-k 64 --warmup-iters 1 --timed-iters 3 --correctness-atol 0.2 --allow-large-materialization --include-dotk-atomic --summary-path /workspace/run_artifacts/sparse_mla_sasg_detached_scatter_s4096_layer2_20260603.json --json

decision: Detaching ScatterAdd into a standalone Triton kernel is correct and gives a small single-shot reduction-stage win over PyTorch sum, but it does not close the S4096 sparse-attention backward gap. The S4096 split remains about 8.75 ms because the producer still writes a dense per-seq-tile partial slab with the raw KxD contribution volume. Host-level ping-pong overlap is negative at this granularity. Keep this as a clean decomposition/measurement rung, but the next production kernel still needs in-tile grouped or segmented accumulation that reduces repeated compressed-group contributions before global memory, ideally fused with the query-major sparse MLA/LI backward body.

ascend_mapping.producer: Cube345-style dKV contribution math emits partial rows.
ascend_mapping.detached_scatter: Standalone Triton ScatterAdd/reduce kernel consumes partial rows and accumulates grad_kv.
ascend_mapping.gap: The AMD split mimics the Ascend stage boundary, but not yet the Ascend CV-pipeline behavior or tile-local coalesced accumulation efficiency.
